message:
feat(fuzz): seccomp + perf_event_open security syscall harness

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_security_syscalls.rs covers
seccomp(2) introspection ops and perf_event_open(2) attr-struct
parsing. 32-byte guard bands around a 192-byte attr buffer (>=
sizeof(struct perf_event_attr) on all kernels we target) verified
intact post-call.

A first-iteration self-caught harness bug: SECCOMP_SET_MODE_STRICT
actually applies STRICT mode to the fuzzer process and kills it on the
next non-RW syscall via DEADLYSIGNAL. Removed STRICT (and the FILTER
op with attacker-controlled BPF) from the op list before ship; only
introspection ops + invalid sentinels remain. Same caution would
apply if/when we add a tracee process for the SET-mode paths.

Verified via rch with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_cc_sec:
5.9M runs at 97K exec/s over 60s, no crashes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
